title: betula × intermedia var. alpestris
intro: Betula × intermedia var. alpestris, also known as the Birch-White Birch, is a hybrid deciduous tree that is a cross between the paper birch (Betula papyrifera) and the silver birch (Betula pendula). The medicinal properties of Betula × intermedia var. alpestris lie in its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, making it a natural anti-inflammatory and analgesic. The health benefits of this plant include reducing pain and inflammation, as well as acting as an antiseptic and diuretic. The bark can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
